title: Dynamic Server Pages for the Mobile Devices
breadcrumbs: Dynamic Server Pages for the Mobile Devices
text: Dynamic Server Pages for the Mobile Devices Dynamic server page (DSP) components enable you to develop responsive user interfaces for web applications and mobile apps. Uniface DSPs have features that are especially designed for mobile device support: Mobile App Layout—a framework that controls how content, menus, and navigation tools are defined for rendering on multiple devices, and a mechanism for binding these to DSP components. For more information, see Mobile App Layout . HTML5 controls—most DSP widgets are implemented as HTML5 controls and support CSS3 styles, enabling superior rendering in mobile web browsers. For more information, see Widgets for Dynamic Server Pages . Custom class attributes—cascading stylesheets enable you to dynamically style the data. Uniface enables you to add your own class attributes to the HTML it generates for the user interface so that you can entirely customize the styling. For more information, see class:ClassName . Syntax error elements and styles—you can customize syntax error handling. Bound error elements can be inserted into web layouts to display syntax errors, and pre-defined styles enable you to display and style those errors as desired. This makes it possible to handle errors differently on mobile devices than in web applications. For more information, see Syntax Checking in Dynamic Server Pages . QR code generation for DSP URLs—in the Uniface IDE, you can generate a QR code for the DSP's URL, so that you can scan and run the DSP on your preferred mobile device. For more information, see Testing DSPs on Mobile Devices . Related Links Dynamic Server Pages Dynamic Server Pages(DSP) components define the user interface of web pages that can be incrementally and dynamically updated. They can be used in rich interactive web applications and in cross-platform mobile apps. Constructing Components and Other Objects During the construction phase of application development activity, you create development objects that define the application components and runtime libraries needed for the application. Application components can reuse definitions that you created in the modeling phase. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
